parentQuestion: A patient with Fanconi syndrome has defective kidney tubules that cannot reabsor...

scaffolds.q: What is the primary function of the kidney tubules?
scaffolds.explain: Kidney tubules reabsorb useful substances (water, glucose, amino acids, electrolytes) from the filtrate back into the blood. This prevents loss of valuable nutrients and helps concentrate waste into urine.

scaffolds.q: What type of epithelium lines most kidney tubules?
scaffolds.explain: Most kidney tubules are lined by simple cuboidal epithelium, which is specialized for absorption and secretion. These cube-shaped cells have enough cytoplasm to house the molecular machinery needed for active transport.
